_meta.source: HUB24 Shadow Portfolio Performance Report + Claude Cowork
_meta.data_notes: Portfolio constituent returns from HUB24 report (01/05/26-27/05/26). ASX 300 TR benchmark from page 1 of HUB24 report. VGS, QUAL, VVLU, IFRA and REIT returns taken directly from HUB24 report (held in portfolios). VGAD approximately +4.48% and VISM approximately +1.39% sourced from web (not in HUB24 report - treat as indicative). Sector index MTD returns (XFJ, XMJ, XEJ, XHJ, XIJ, XSJ, XDJ, XTJ, XPJ, XUJ) and fixed income ETF benchmarks (AAA, IAF, VAGB) could not be verified and are set to null.

aus_holdings.news: FY26 EBITDA guidance narrowed to $2.06-$2.18b; K2 gas peaker FID; Liddell Battery on track. Analyst consensus target cut to ~$11.48; Ord Minnett cautious on energy market transition dynamics.
Q3 FY26 (6 May): net sales +77% to US$5.91b on Berry acquisition; adjusted EBITDA +87%; EPS slightly missed; FY26 guidance raised. CDI dividend of AUD 91c paid.
Big Four banks retreated sharply in May; ANZ outperformed peers (down 4.8%) amid sector contagion from NAB earnings miss, increased provisions, and federal budget housing concerns.
Q3 FY26: iron ore output +1.7% YoY to 62.8Mt; copper -7.1% to 476.8kt; full-year guidance maintained with copper tracking toward upper end of range.
No material news in the period.
Shares fell 10% intraday on 13 May - largest single-session fall on record - on federal budget housing measures and sector contagion from NAB earnings miss. Partially recovered by month end.
No material news in the period.
Q3 FY26 sales update: group revenue +3.1% to $10.7b; supermarket comp sales +3.6% (ex-tobacco +5.7%); eCommerce +24.8%; Liquor division remains challenged.
2026 Strategy Refresh Investor Briefing scheduled; analyst consensus price target maintained at ~A$2.99. No material operational news in the period.
Q3 FY26: platform FUA $127.8b (+25% YoY); total FUA $151.7b (+22%); net inflows $4.0b; super trustee acquisition progressing pending regulatory approval.
Secured A$120m in new contracts: Rio Tinto 5-year Pilbara crane/lifting panel, Fortescue Cloudbreak battery storage project, Port Waratah 3-year maintenance panel.
FY26 (8 May): NPAT +30% to $4.85b, beat expectations; Commodities & Global Markets +49%; deposits +25% to $221.5b. Morgan Stanley upgraded to Overweight, target $270. Final dividend declared.
H1 cash earnings missed consensus; flagged uncertain asset-quality outlook amid Iran conflict and energy price shock; increased credit provisions, weighing on sector sentiment.
No material news in the period.
No material news in the period.
Ord Minnett trimmed battery earnings estimates for FY27-28; shares fell sharply amid broader ASX energy sector weakness driven by geopolitical tensions and lower oil prices.
H1 FY26 (7 May): record EBIT $512m (+5%); NPAT +8%; dividend +14% to $0.285; $100m cost savings program launched; acquired Nelson Brothers explosives. Shares surged ~9% on results day.
Q1 trading update: gross premiums +11% to US$9.2b; FY26 guidance reaffirmed; disaster claims tracking hot at US$300m vs US$517m half-year budget; market cautious on softening pricing cycle.
Q1 FY26: Pilbara iron ore +13% YoY (2nd highest Q1 since 2018); copper +9% to 229kt; first Simandou high-grade shipment delivered to China. FY26 guidance unchanged.
Q3 FY26 (30 Apr): revenue +11%; non-GAAP EPS $2.86 beat $2.81 consensus; gross margin +290bps. Shares fell ~7% on CFO transition announcement and $340m Noctrix acquisition. CDI ex-div 13 May.
Unmarketable parcel buyback announced (51,621 shares at $4.11, record date 11 May). No material operational news in the period.
Q3 FY26 operational update: Masterplanned Communities sales +43% YoY; Land Lease Communities also lifted; FY26 guidance maintained.
Passive ASX 200 exposure; return broadly tracks index performance. No material news in the period.
H1 FY26 (19 May): 17th consecutive record result; ARR +17% to $598m; UK ARR +23% to $53m; FY26 PBT growth guidance reaffirmed at 18-20%. Shares fell ~4% on results day on valuation concerns.
AGM results of meeting released 8 May; shares under pressure. No material operational news in the period.
Geopolitical tensions and oil price weakness continued to weigh on ASX energy sector; analyst sentiment broadly cautious on near-term earnings outlook.
FY26 (14 May): revenue +31% to $2.75b; adjusted EBITDA +18%, ~2% ahead of consensus; NPAT -27% due to Melio acquisition costs. RBC downgraded to Underperform. Shares fell 7.82%.

ge_holdings.commentary: T. Rowe Price Global Equity outperformed VGS (+5.40%) by +0.66% and the QUAL factor (+5.01%) by +1.05%. The fund's quality-growth bias was rewarded as global equities rallied broadly. Active fundamental stock selection across developed markets added value over the passive benchmark, consistent with the manager's bottom-up investment approach.

Arrowstreet Global Equity Hedged returned +6.30%, outperforming its VGAD benchmark (+4.48%) by +1.82% and unhedged VGS (+5.40%) by +0.90%. The fund's systematic factor approach combining value, momentum, and earnings revision signals captured the global rally effectively. Currency hedging provided added stability versus the unhedged benchmark.

Arrowstreet Global Small Companies returned +2.40%, ahead of the VISM small-cap benchmark (+1.39%) by +1.01%, though significantly behind large-cap VGS (+5.40%). Global small caps underperformed large caps as market leadership concentrated in mega-cap technology names. Active factor signals added relative value within the small-cap universe.

Aoris International returned +0.64%, materially behind VGS (+5.40%) and QUAL (+5.01%). The fund's concentrated, quality-focused portfolio favouring companies with durable competitive advantages was a headwind in a month where broad momentum and technology mega-caps drove index returns. The fund's selective, low-turnover approach prioritises long-term capital preservation over index participation.
VanEck MSCI International Quality ETF returned +5.01%, reflecting strong performance from quality-factor international equities over the period.
Vanguard MSCI Index International Shares ETF returned +5.40%, tracking broad developed-market international equity index performance for the period.
Vanguard Global Value Equity Active ETF returned +2.80%, lagging VGS as value-style equities underperformed growth and momentum stocks globally over the month.

pi_holdings.commentary: Ausbil Global Infrastructure Hedged returned -1.33%, underperforming the IFRA benchmark (+1.80%) by -3.13%. The fund's active positioning in energy infrastructure and regulated utilities detracted as rate-sensitive infrastructure assets faced valuation pressure from shifting interest rate expectations during the month. Currency hedging broadly neutralised FX effects over the period.

Quay Global Real Estate (Unhedged) returned +0.28%, underperforming the hedged REIT benchmark (+3.42%) by -3.14%. A meaningful portion of this gap reflects the structural currency difference: REIT is AUD-hedged while Quay carries full foreign currency exposure, and AUD strength over the period was a headwind to unhedged returns. Quay's concentrated quality portfolio also had less exposure to US REIT sub-sectors driving the broader index rally.

ATLAS Infrastructure Hedged returned +0.73%, trailing the IFRA benchmark (+1.80%) by -1.07%. The fund's concentrated core infrastructure holdings - airports, toll roads, and regulated utilities across developed markets - delivered modest but stable returns. Currency hedging neutralised FX translation effects; relative underperformance reflects the fund's more defensive sector composition versus the broader benchmark.
VanEck FTSE Global Infrastructure ETF returned +1.80%, reflecting modest positive performance from global listed infrastructure assets over the period.

VanEck FTSE International Property ETF (AUD-hedged) returned +3.42%, with global listed real estate outperforming broader infrastructure over the period. The hedged structure eliminated foreign currency translation risk for the month.

fi_holdings.commentary: Bentham Global Income returned +1.17%, generating attractive income-driven returns from its diversified global credit portfolio. Exposure to floating-rate credit instruments across investment-grade and high-yield markets continued to benefit from elevated global interest rates, with credit spread compression contributing to total return over the period.

Bentham Asset Backed Securities returned +1.34%, the strongest result in the Fixed Interest portfolio. The fund's focus on high-quality, floating-rate ABS benefited from stable credit conditions and attractive carry. Limited duration and high credit quality provided resilience, with the return likely comfortably exceeding the bank-bill proxy benchmark over the period.

Macquarie Dynamic Bond returned +0.53%, reflecting its dynamic allocation across global fixed income instruments. The fund's flexible mandate allows active duration and sector positioning; the moderate return suggests a more cautious duration stance in a month where longer-dated bonds broadly outperformed shorter-term instruments as global rate expectations shifted.

Perpetual Diversified Income returned +0.52%, driven by carry income from a diversified credit portfolio spanning investment-grade bonds and floating-rate securities. The fund's broad mandate allows active management of credit quality and duration, with returns for the period broadly reflecting income generation from high-quality domestic and global credit exposures.

Coolabah Short Term Income returned +0.34%, consistent with its objective of exceeding the RBA cash rate proxy. The fund's portfolio of short-duration Australian cash and bond securities delivered stable income yield, with near-zero interest rate duration providing effective insulation from bond price volatility over the period.
